Stocked SKUs18
Capital at stake$869K
Portfolio GMROI2.8×

Top-right is cash: Tee, Candle and Tote turn fast at strong margin — scale them. Bottom-left is dust: Cutlery and Mug earn nothing and freeze $44K. The pulsing rings are what Magellan is flagging — amber is about to stock out, red is dead.

Operations AI · Velocity
Live3 signalsSynced just now
Velocity spike
Organic Tee accelerating +47%
Daily velocity jumped from 14 to 24 over 7 days — fast and high-margin (45%). At this pace its 89-day cover halves. <b>Reorder before it gaps</b>, and tell Growth it has headroom to scale.
Stockout prediction
2 fast movers under 1.5 weeks
USB-C Cable and Screen Protector are turning fast with thin cover — <b>3 and 4 days</b> of stock against 5-day lead times. Both will gap before a reorder lands.
Don't reorder yet
Three you shouldn't reorder yet
Wool Beanie (−55%, seasonal), Linen Apron and Notebook already carry <b>71–200 days</b> of cover. Velocity is fading or stock is deep — that capital is better deployed in ads, not more units.
Velocity momentum — accelerating vs decelerating · 30-day change
8accelerating · scale
10decelerating · hold
Decline reads as loud as growththe honest operator also says wait

Eight SKUs are speeding up and ten slowing down. The green side is where to lean in; the red side is where every other tool would still tell you to buy — and you should wait. Wool Beanie at −55% is a seasonal fade, not a restock cue.

MConsole note
Read
18 stocked SKUs · portfolio GMROI 2.8×, above the 2.5× target. 8 are accelerating, 10 decelerating, and $44K sits in 2 dead lines turning at near-zero.
Exposure
Two fast movers (Cable, Screen Protector) will stock out inside a week. At the same time, Notebook, Beanie and Apron carry 71–200 days of cover — buying more of those would freeze cash in stock that is slowing, not selling.
Directive
Reorder the accelerating fast movers — Tee, Cable, Screen. Do not reorder Notebook, Beanie or Apron: velocity is fading or cover is deep, so that capital earns more in ads. Clear Bamboo Cutlery and Enamel Mug — they are dust, not stock.
Ask Magellan anything…
